column1: # Memory scope policy for scoped-prime.sh (the per-agent memory filter).
# Whitespace-separated. Blank lines and lines starting with '#' are ignored.
#   default <db-prefix> <owner>    Default owner for LEGACY (un-namespaced)
#                                  memories in the db with that issue_prefix.
#   key     <memory-key> <owner>   Override for one specific legacy memory.
# Resolution order for a memory's owner (first match wins):
#   1. A namespaced key "<owner>:<slug>" (e.g. captain:foo, shared:bar) — the
#      prefix before ':' IS the owner. NEW memories should be written this way
#      (see the agent templates) and need no entry in this file.
#   2. A 'key' override row below.
#   3. The 'default' owner for the memory's db.
# An agent primes only the memories whose owner is itself OR 'shared'.
# owners: captain | shipwright | economy-analyst | surveyor |
#         harbormaster | shared
# sp = rig db (repo-root .beads): the captain's operational memory store, so
#      legacy lessons there default to the captain.
# st = city db (city/.beads): city/crew governance, defaults to shared.
default  sp  captain
default  st  shared
# --- legacy overrides (memories that differ from their db default) --------
# Cross-cutting directives in the sp (captain) db that ALL crew should honor:
key  admiral-afk-never-a-dependency                   shared
key  bd-quirk-bd-init-prefix-x-sets-only              shared
key  spacetraders-cli-ops-era-2-verified-always-run   shared
# Agent-private memories in the st (shared-default) db (scoped by the agent
# named in the key; the admiral-*/crew-*/bd-* governance memories stay shared
# via the default). New memories should namespace their own keys instead.
key  shipwright-session-beads-not-tasks               shipwright
key  i-am-shipwright-mail-addressed-to-shipwright-is  shipwright
key  harbormaster-session-has-two-mail-aliases-harbormaster-and  harbormaster
